questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If 'MINE' is coded as 'NJOF', then 'COAL' is coded as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each letter is shifted forward by one (the +1 cipher).

**Working.** C→D, O→P, A→B, L→M gives DPBM.

**Answer.** DPBM.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a code, 'ROCK' is written as 'QNBJ'. Using the same rule, 'SAND' is written as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Determine the shift: R→Q is $-1$.

**Working.** Each letter shifts back by one: S→R, A→Z, N→M, D→C gives RZMC.

**Answer.** RZMC.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a standard die (opposite faces sum to $7$), the top shows $1$ and the front shows $2$. The number on the bottom face is _____ .

figure.caption: A die showing one pip on top and two pips on the front face.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='30,40 80,40 100,25 50,25' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.12)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='30' y='40' width='50' height='55' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='55' cy='32' r='4' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='45' cy='58' r='4' fill='#334155'/><circle cx='65' cy='78' r='4' fill='#334155'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bottom $=7-$ top.

**Working.** $7-1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The units digit of $7^{4}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Units digits of powers of $7$ cycle $7, 9, 3, 1$ with period $4$.

**Working.** $7^4$ ends the cycle at $1$ (indeed $7^4=2401$).

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Five years ago, a man was seven times as old as his son. The man is now $40$ years old. The son's present age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Translate the past condition into an equation.

**Working.** Five years ago the man was $35$, so $35=7(\text{son}-5)\Rightarrow \text{son}-5=5\Rightarrow \text{son}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Interior angle $=\dfrac{(n-2)\times 180}{n}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{(6-2)\times 180}{6}=\dfrac{720}{6}=120^\circ$.

**Answer.** $120$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Statement: 'All safety helmets in the store are yellow.' Conclusion: 'No safety helmet in the store is blue.' Does the conclusion follow?
options: No, the conclusion does not follow
The conclusion is the opposite of the statement
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A thing of one exclusive colour cannot be another colour.

**Working.** If every helmet is yellow, none can be blue; the conclusion follows.

**Answer.** Yes, the conclusion follows.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An arrow rotates $90^\circ$ clockwise each step: up, right, down, ... The next arrow points

figure.caption: Three arrows pointing up, right and down, followed by a question mark.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the $90^\circ$ clockwise rotation.

**Working.** After down, a further clockwise quarter-turn points to the left.

**Answer.** Left (west).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the dot series shown ($1, 2, 3, \ldots$ dots), the next figure should contain

figure.caption: Three groups of dots containing one, two and three dots respectively.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='95' cy='50' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='165' cy='25' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='165' cy='45' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='165' cy='65' r='6' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='210' y='45' font-size='28' fill='#94a3b8'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The count increases by one each step.

**Working.** $1, 2, 3, \ldots$ so the next figure has $4$ dots.

**Answer.** 4 dots.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The number of positive divisors of $36$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $N=p^a q^b$, the divisor count is $(a+1)(b+1)$.

**Working.** $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$, so $(2+1)(2+1)=9$.

**Answer.** $9$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A caving mine has 50 active drawpoints, each drawing 500 t/day. The daily production, in t, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ drawpoints $\times$ tonnage per drawpoint.

**Working.**
$$Q=50\times500=25{,}000\ \text{t/day}$$

**Answer.** 25,000 t/day.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following are methods for finding ROOTS of equations?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Root-finding vs numerical integration.

**Working.**
- Bisection, Newton–Raphson, secant — all root-finding (TRUE).
- Trapezoidal rule — numerical integration (FALSE).

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: A PERT activity has optimistic $4$, most-likely $7$ and pessimistic $16$ days (figure). Compute its expected time (days).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $t_e=\dfrac{o+4m+p}{6}$.

**Working.**
$$t_e=\frac{4+4\times7+16}{6}=\frac{48}{6}=8\ \text{days}$$

**Answer.** $8$ days.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Methane is released into mine workings mainly by
options: desorption of gas from the coal seam and surrounding strata as they are exposed and relieved of pressure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Coal stores adsorbed methane.

**Reasoning.** Mining relieves the confining pressure, so methane desorbs from the freshly exposed coal and broken strata and emits into the airways; the rate rises with gas content and production.

**Answer.** Desorption from coal/strata on pressure relief.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Most main mine fans are operated as exhaust (induced) fans, which keep the workings
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An exhausting fan draws air out of the upcast.

**Working.** This holds the workings just below atmospheric pressure, aiding gas drainage.

**Answer.** slightly below atmospheric pressure.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplace transform $\mathcal{L}\{t\}$ evaluated at $s=2$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A truck climbs a 5% grade as shown, with a rolling resistance of 20 kg/t. Taking grade resistance as 10 kg/t per 1% of grade, compute the total resistance (kg/t).

figure.caption: Truck on grade
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='220' y2='40' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='120' y='52' width='34' height='18' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569' transform='rotate(-18 137 61)'/><text x='30' y='115' font-size='8'>grade 5%, rolling res 20 kg/t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total resistance = rolling resistance + grade resistance.

**Working.**
$$R = 20 + 10 \times 5 = 20 + 50 = 70$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{70}$ kg/t.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If an airway's cross-sectional area is reduced to half while the quantity stays constant, the air velocity
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=Av$ is constant, so $v\propto1/A$.

**Working.** Halving $A$ doubles $v$.

**Answer.** doubles.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: As the humidity of mine air rises, the body's ability to lose heat by sweating
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Evaporation slows as air approaches saturation.

**Working.** High humidity reduces sweat evaporation and cooling.

**Answer.** decreases.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the conservative field $\mathbf{F}=\nabla\phi$ with $\phi=xy$, the line integral $\int_{C}\mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r}$ along the path $C$ from $(0,0)$ to $(2,3)$ is _____ .

figure.caption: A path C from (0,0) to (2,3); the integral of a gradient field is path-independent.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='145' x2='185' y2='145' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='20' x2='25' y2='145' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M25 145 Q90 60 150 50' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='25' cy='145' r='3' fill='#10b981'/><circle cx='150' cy='50' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='30' y='160' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>(0,0)</text><text x='130' y='42' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>(2,3)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a gradient field, $\int_C\nabla\phi\cdot d\mathbf{r}=\phi(\text{end})-\phi(\text{start})$ (path-independent).

**Working.**
$$\phi(2,3)-\phi(0,0)=(2)(3)-0=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the uniaxial stress of $120\,\text{MPa}$, the shear stress on the plane whose normal makes $30^\circ$ with the stress axis is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau=\sigma\sin\theta\cos\theta$.

**Working.**
$$\tau=120\sin 30^\circ\cos 30^\circ=120(0.5)(0.866)=51.96.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 51.96\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Two cross-sections 30 m apart have areas 20 m² and 40 m². By the average end-area (trapezoidal) method the volume, in m³, is ____.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For any twice-differentiable scalar field $f$, the quantity $\nabla\times(\nabla f)$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The curl of a gradient is identically zero.

**Working.**
$$\nabla\times(\nabla f)=\mathbf{0}$$
This is why every gradient field is irrotational.

**Answer.** $\mathbf{0}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers an air power of $18\,\text{kW}$ while its shaft power is $22.5\,\text{kW}$. The fan efficiency is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{\text{air power}}{\text{shaft power}}$.

**Working.**
$$\eta=\frac{18}{22.5}=0.80.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $y'=y$ with $y(0)=1$ and step $h=0.1$, one Euler step gives $y(0.1)\approx$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The order of $\dfrac{d^{3}y}{dx^{3}}+x\left(\dfrac{dy}{dx}\right)^{4}=\sin x$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order is the highest derivative, regardless of any powers.

**Working.** The highest derivative is $y'''$, so the order is $3$ (the exponent $4$ would be the degree of the first-derivative term, not the order).

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In the normal-stress versus shear-stress plane, the Mohr-Coulomb failure envelope is
options: a straight line of slope $\tan\phi$ and intercept $c$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau=c+\sigma_n\tan\phi$ is linear in $\sigma_n$.

**Working.** Slope $=\tan\phi$, intercept $=c$.

**Answer.** a straight line of slope $\tan\phi$ and intercept $c$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A line is measured as 500 m with a 20 m chain that is found to be 0.05 m too long. The corrected (true) length, in m, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** True length $=$ measured $\times\dfrac{\text{actual chain length}}{\text{nominal length}}$.

**Working.**
$$L=500\times\frac{20.05}{20}=500\times1.0025=501.25\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 501.25 m.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Wedge failure of a rock slope involves sliding
options: along the line of intersection of two discontinuity planes
along a single horizontal bedding plane
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two intersecting planes form a wedge.

**Working.** The wedge slides along their line of intersection.

**Answer.** along the line of intersection of two discontinuity planes.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: As the extraction ratio of a room-and-pillar panel increases, the pillar stress
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_p=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{1-e}$.

**Working.** Larger $e$ shrinks $1-e$, so $\sigma_p$ rises.

**Answer.** increases.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $500\,\text{Pa}$ while passing $40\,\text{m}^3$/s. The air power is _____ W.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=P\,Q$.

**Working.**
$$500\times40=20000.$$

**Answer.** $20{,}000\,\text{W}=20\,\text{kW}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For $\sigma_x=50$, $\sigma_y=10$, $\tau_{xy}=15\,\text{MPa}$, the minor principal stress $\sigma_2$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_2=c-R$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_2=30-25=5.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The single most critical requirement for successful block caving is that the ore
options: is extremely hard and massive with no joints
caves and fragments readily once the support is removed by undercutting
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Caving relies on the rock breaking itself.

**Reasoning.** Adequate caveability — governed by jointing, strength and stress — ensures the block propagates upward and fragments to a drawable size after undercutting. Massive, unjointed ore may not cave or may produce oversize.

**Answer.** It must cave/fragment readily after undercutting.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Multi-rope friction winders are favoured for very deep shafts because
options: the load is shared among several ropes, allowing smaller rope diameter and compact, high-capacity deep winding
they eliminate the need for braking
they avoid the use of any sheave
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Spreading load across ropes overcomes single-rope limits.

**Reasoning.** At great depth a single rope would be impractically heavy/large; sharing the load over 4–6 ropes keeps each rope small and the traction sheave compact, enabling deep, high-capacity hoisting.

**Answer.** Load shared across ropes for compact deep winding.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Stone dusting (rock dusting) of coal-mine roadways prevents coal-dust explosions by
options: mixing incombustible dust (e.g. limestone) with the coal dust so the cloud cannot propagate an explosion
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inert dust quenches the flame.

**Reasoning.** Spreading limestone dust raises the incombustible fraction of the roadway dust; when raised into a cloud, the inert particles absorb heat and dilute the coal dust below the level that can sustain an explosion.

**Answer.** Adds incombustible dust to suppress propagation.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A water-filled tension crack at the crest of a slope generally
options: reduces stability by adding a driving water thrust
has no effect on the factor of safety
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Water pressure in the crack pushes the block outward.

**Working.** It adds a destabilising force, lowering FoS.

**Answer.** reduces stability by adding a driving water thrust.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If a fan's speed is increased by $10\%$, the pressure increases by _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P\propto N^2$.

**Working.**
$$1.1^2=1.21\Rightarrow +21\%.$$

**Answer.** $21\%$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The function of stemming (inert material placed in the collar of the hole) is to
options: increase the VOD of the explosive
confine the detonation gases so they do their work on the rock, reducing airblast and flyrock
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stemming confines the borehole.

**Reasoning.** Drill cuttings/angular gravel in the collar resist the gas pressure long enough for the explosive energy to fragment and heave the rock instead of venting up the hole — cutting airblast and flyrock.

**Answer.** Confine gases; reduce airblast/flyrock.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Inertisation of a sealed-off fire area with nitrogen or CO₂ controls the fire by
options: lowering the oxygen content below the level that supports combustion and explosion
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combustion needs oxygen above a threshold.

**Reasoning.** Injecting inert gas dilutes the oxygen in the sealed zone below the level needed for burning/explosion, suppressing the fire without water and reducing explosion risk.

**Answer.** Reduces oxygen below the combustion threshold.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Using $\rho=\dfrac{P}{RT}$ with $P=100{,}000\,\text{Pa}$, $R=287\,\text{J/kg\,K}$ and $T=300\,\text{K}$, the air density is _____ kg/m$^3$. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ideal-gas density $\rho=\dfrac{P}{RT}$.

**Working.**
$$\rho=\frac{100000}{287\times300}=\frac{100000}{86100}=1.16.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.16\,\text{kg/m}^3$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A cooling duty of 251.25 kW is required. Taking 1 tonne of refrigeration (TR) = 3.517 kW, the refrigeration capacity needed, in TR, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{TR}=\dfrac{\dot{Q}\ (\text{kW})}{3.517}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{TR}=\frac{251.25}{3.517}=71.4\ \text{TR}$$

**Answer.** 71.4 TR.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A plane truss has $m=15$ members, $j=8$ joints and $r=4$ reaction components. The degree of static indeterminacy ($m+r-2j$) is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Degree of indeterminacy $=m+r-2j$.

**Working.**
$$15+4-2(8)=19-16=3.$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In a triaxial compression test, increasing the confining pressure generally
options: increases the peak strength and ductility
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Confinement suppresses crack opening.

**Working.** Higher $\sigma_3$ raises peak strength and promotes ductile behaviour.

**Answer.** increases the peak strength and ductility.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two parallel branches share a pressure drop of $200\,\text{Pa}$. The branch of resistance $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\sqrt{P/R}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\sqrt{\frac{200}{0.5}}=\sqrt{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Triangulation as a method of horizontal control depends on measuring
options: the angles of a network of triangles together with at least one precisely measured baseline
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angles plus a baseline fix the network.

**Reasoning.** With one measured baseline and the observed angles, the sine rule yields all other sides; redundant angles provide checks. It was the classic method for extensive control.

**Answer.** Angles + one measured baseline.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: In the activity network shown, the upper path durations are $6$ and $9$ days and the lower path durations are $4$ and $5$ days between the same start and end nodes. Compute the project (critical-path) duration in days.

figure.caption: Activity network
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='14'/><circle cx='110' cy='40' r='14'/><circle cx='110' cy='100' r='14'/><circle cx='200' cy='70' r='14'/></g><g stroke='#475569' marker-end='url(#na)'><line x1='44' y1='65' x2='96' y2='45'/><line x1='44' y1='75' x2='96' y2='95'/><line x1='124' y1='45' x2='186' y2='65'/><line x1='124' y1='95' x2='186' y2='75'/></g><defs><marker id='na' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='7' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='62' y='40' font-size='8'>6</text><text x='62' y='118' font-size='8'>4</text><text x='160' y='42' font-size='8'>9</text><text x='160' y='112' font-size='8'>5</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The critical path is the longest path through the network.

**Working.**
$$\text{upper}=6+9=15,\quad \text{lower}=4+5=9\Rightarrow \text{duration}=15$$

**Answer.** $15$ days.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock pillar with compressive strength $120\,\text{MPa}$ carries an axial stress of $40\,\text{MPa}$. Its factor of safety is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\text{strength}}{\text{stress}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{120}{40}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A coal seam covers an area of 1,000,000 m² with an average thickness of 3 m and an in-situ density of 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ tonnage, in million tonnes, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ area $\times$ thickness $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$T=1{,}000{,}000\times3\times1.4=4.2\times10^{6}\ \text{t}=4.2\ \text{Mt}$$

**Answer.** 4.2 million tonnes.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A constant force of $10\,\text{N}$ acts for $4\,\text{s}$ on a $5\,\text{kg}$ body initially at rest. Its final speed is _____ m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Impulse $=Ft=m\,\Delta v$.

**Working.**
$$\Delta v=\frac{Ft}{m}=\frac{10\times 4}{5}=8.$$

**Answer.** $8\,\text{m/s}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A cylindrical cartridge of 32 mm diameter and 200 mm length has an explosive density of 1200 kg/m³. Its mass, in grams, is ____ (nearest gram).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Downcast air has density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$ and upcast air $1.1\,\text{kg/m}^3$ over a shaft depth of $200\,\text{m}$. The natural ventilation pressure ($\text{NVP}=(\rho_d-\rho_u)gH$, $g=9.81$) is _____ Pa. Round to one decimal.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{NVP}=(\rho_d-\rho_u)\,g\,H$.

**Working.**
$$(1.2-1.1)\times9.81\times200=0.1\times9.81\times200=196.2.$$

**Answer.** $196.2\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Square pillars $8\,\text{m}$ wide are separated by $4\,\text{m}$ openings under $\sigma_v=20\,\text{MPa}$. The pillar stress $\sigma_v\left(\dfrac{W_p+W_o}{W_p}\right)^2$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Square-pillar tributary formula.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=20\left(\frac{12}{8}\right)^2=20(2.25)=45.$$

**Answer.** $45\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The general solution of the Cauchy–Euler equation $x^{2}y''-3xy'+3y=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Try $y=x^{m}$; this yields an indicial equation.

**Working.**
$$m(m-1)-3m+3=m^{2}-4m+3=(m-1)(m-3)=0\Rightarrow m=1,3$$
Solution $C_1 x+C_2 x^{3}$.

**Answer.** $C_1 x+C_2 x^{3}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air of density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$ flows at $50\,\text{m}^3$/s. The mass flow rate is _____ kg/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dot m=\rho Q$.

**Working.**
$$1.2\times50=60.$$

**Answer.** $60\,\text{kg/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project costs $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$ and returns $\textsf{Rs}\,20{,}000$ per year for $4$ years. At a MARR of $12\%$ (so $(P/A,12\%,4)=3.0373$), its NPV is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: By the ratio test, the power series $\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}\frac{x^{n}}{n!}$ converges for
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ratio test: $\lim\left|\dfrac{a_{n+1}}{a_n}\right|<1$ for convergence.

**Working.**
$$\left|\frac{x^{n+1}/(n+1)!}{x^{n}/n!}\right|=\frac{|x|}{n+1}\to 0<1\quad\forall x$$
The radius of convergence is infinite (this series is $e^{x}$).

**Answer.** All real $x$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a long rib pillar, $\sigma_p=\sigma_v\dfrac{W_p+W_o}{W_p}$. With $W_p=8\,\text{m}$, $W_o=4\,\text{m}$ and $\sigma_v=20\,\text{MPa}$, the pillar stress is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rib pillars use the linear (not squared) width ratio.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=20\times\frac{12}{8}=20(1.5)=30.$$

**Answer.** $30\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: If the ratio $N=\sigma_c/\sigma_t=4$ and $N=\tan^2(45^\circ+\phi/2)$, the friction angle $\phi$ is _____ degrees. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tan(45^\circ+\phi/2)=\sqrt{N}$.

**Working.**
$$\sqrt{4}=2,\quad 45^\circ+\frac{\phi}{2}=\tan^{-1}2=63.43^\circ,\quad \phi=36.87^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 36.87^\circ$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The elastic strain-energy density at failure is $U=\dfrac{\sigma^2}{2E}$. For $\sigma=100\,\text{MPa}$ and $E=50\,\text{GPa}$, $U$ in kJ/m$^3$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $U=\dfrac{\sigma^2}{2E}$.

**Working.**
$$U=\frac{(100\times10^6)^2}{2(50\times10^9)}=\frac{1\times10^{16}}{1\times10^{11}}=1\times10^5\,\text{J/m}^3=100\,\text{kJ/m}^3.$$

**Answer.** $100\,\text{kJ/m}^3$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Underground metal mining methods are broadly classified as
options: unsupported (naturally supported), supported, and caving methods
shallow, medium and deep methods only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The classic classification is by ground-support strategy.

**Reasoning.** Methods are grouped as naturally supported/open (e.g. sublevel and room-and-pillar), artificially supported (e.g. cut-and-fill, square-set) and caving (e.g. block and sublevel caving).

**Answer.** Unsupported, supported and caving methods.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the intact Hoek-Brown criterion with $\sigma_c=100\,\text{MPa}$, $m=10$, $s=1$ and $\sigma_3=10\,\text{MPa}$, $\sigma_1$ is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_1=\sigma_3+\sigma_c\sqrt{m\sigma_3/\sigma_c+s}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_1=10+100\sqrt{\frac{10\times10}{100}+1}=10+100\sqrt{2}=10+141.42=151.42.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 151.42\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A vane anemometer is used to measure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The vane rotates in proportion to air speed.

**Working.** It reads the velocity of the airflow.

**Answer.** air velocity.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: For the PERT activity with $o=4$ and $p=8$ days, compute the variance of the activity time.

figure.caption: PERT variance from spread
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='210' y2='80' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='70' cy='80' r='4' fill='#16a34a'/><circle cx='170' cy='80' r='4' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='55' y='70' font-size='8'>o=4</text><text x='158' y='70' font-size='8'>p=8</text><line x1='70' y1='95' x2='170' y2='95' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PERT variance $=\left(\dfrac{p-o}{6}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma^2=\left(\frac{8-4}{6}\right)^2=\left(0.667\right)^2=0.444$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.44$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: Electronic detonators differ from pyrotechnic-delay detonators chiefly in offering
options: highly accurate, programmable delay times with negligible scatter
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Electronic delays use a microchip timer.

**Reasoning.** Instead of a burning pyrotechnic element (which scatters), a programmable chip gives precise, freely chosen delays with millisecond accuracy, allowing optimised timing for fragmentation and vibration control.

**Answer.** Accurate, programmable delays.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using tributary area theory with a vertical stress of 5 MPa and an extraction ratio of 0.36, the average pillar stress, in MPa, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_p=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{1-e}$ (tributary area theory).

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=\frac{5}{1-0.36}=\frac{5}{0.64}=7.81\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** 7.81 MPa.
